translations.en: When 'Utba heard this, he summoned a scribe and wrote: 'In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful. To the servant of Allah, Yazid, Commander of the Faithful; from 'Utba b. Abi Sufyan. As for what follows: al-Husayn b. 'Ali does not recognize your caliphate or your right to a pledge of allegiance. Your view regarding his affair. Peace.' When the letter reached Yazid (may Allah curse him), he wrote the reply to 'Utba: 'As for what follows: When this letter of mine reaches you, hasten to write back to me and clarify in your letter all who are in my obedience and all who have defected. And with the reply, send the head of al-Husayn b. 'Ali.' When al-Husayn (peace be upon him) learned of this, he resolved to leave the land of the Hijaz for Iraq. When night fell, he went to the Mosque of the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) to bid farewell to the grave. When he reached the grave, a light blazed forth from it, and he returned to his place. On the second night, he went to bid farewell to the grave. He stood in prayer and prolonged it. He fell drowsy in prostration. The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) came to him in his sleep, and took al-Husayn (peace be upon him) and pressed him to his chest. He began kissing between his eyes and saying: 'May my father be your ransom! It is as though I see you covered in your blood, between a group from this nation who hope for my intercession — yet Allah has no portion for them. O my son! You are heading toward your father, your mother, and your brother, and they long for you. You have ranks in Paradise that cannot be attained except through martyrdom.'
